title: RAG is dead, right?? — Kuba Rogut, Turbopuffer
summary: Kuba Rogut counters the "RAG is dead" discourse by arguing the problem was never retrieval itself but an overly narrow definition of it. RAG should include vector search, full text search, glob, regex, and filters used iteratively by agents. Real-world usage curves show RAG adoption growing even as Twitter discourse dismisses it.
writeup: Kuba Rogut of Turbopuffer pushes back on the claim that RAG is dead, arguing the issue was a narrow definition of retrieval rather than retrieval itself. The talk reframes RAG to span vector search, full text search, glob, regex, and filters applied iteratively by agents, rather than a single vector-search step. It notes that real-world adoption curves show RAG usage growing even as online discourse dismisses it.
